definition: Immigration has contributed 0.3-0.4pp GDP growth annually (CBO data: ~3.3M net immigration in 2023, adding 3-4M workers above pre-pandemic trend), but this tailwind is decaying due to politically-driven reversal. Executive authority is broadest on immigration, CBP encounters are down sharply, and affected sectors (construction 10-15%, food services 20-25% immigrant workforce) feed directly into services CPI.
evidence: CBO data on 3.3M net immigration in 2023
Immigration double-counting resolved through Kalecki profit equation: immigrants add output while compressing household income share
CBP encounters declining sharply from peak
open_questions: How fast will labor market impact materialize?
What is the actual productivity level of immigrant workers relative to average?
